audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Passion plot, supper, covenant cup, dispute over greatness, Peter warning, Mount of Olives prayer, arrest, Peter denial, mocking, council.

verses.restored: The feast of Unleavened Bread, called Passover, was drawing near.
The chief priests and scribes were seeking how they might remove him, for they feared the people.
The Adversary entered into Judas called Iscariot, who was of the number of the twelve.
He went away and conferred with the chief priests and officers how he might hand him over to them.
They rejoiced and agreed to give him silver.
He consented and sought a good opportunity to hand him over apart from a crowd.
The day of Unleavened Bread came, on which the Passover had to be sacrificed.
He sent Peter and John, saying, Go and prepare the Passover for us, so that we may eat.
They said to him, Where do you want us to prepare?
He said to them, Look, when you enter the city, a person carrying a jar of water will meet you; follow him into the house he enters.
Say to the house-master, The Teacher says to you, Where is the guest room where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?
He will show you a large furnished upper room; prepare there.
They went and found just as he had said to them, and they prepared the Passover.
When the hour came, he reclined, and the apostles with him.
He said to them, With desire I desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
For I say to you, I will certainly not eat it until it is fulfilled in the reign of the Divine.
Taking a cup, giving thanks, he said, Take this and divide it among yourselves.
For I say to you, from now on I will certainly not drink from the fruit of the vine until the reign of the Divine comes.
Taking bread, giving thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying, This is my body, given for you; do this in remembrance of me.
Likewise also the cup after supper, saying, This cup is the new covenant in my blood, poured out for you.
But look, the hand of the one handing me over is with me on the table.
For the Son of Humanity goes according to what has been determined; nevertheless woe to that person through whom he is handed over.
They began to discuss among themselves who of them might be about to do this.
A dispute also arose among them about which of them seemed to be greatest.
He said to them, The kings of the nations lord it over them, and those exercising authority over them are called benefactors.
But not so with you; rather the greater among you must become like the younger, and the leader like the one serving.
For who is greater, the one reclining or the one serving? Is it not the one reclining? But I am among you as the one serving.
You are the ones who have remained with me in my tests.
I appoint to you, just as my Father appointed to me, a reign,
so that you may eat and drink at my table in my reign, and sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
Simon, Simon, look, the Adversary demanded to sift you all like wheat.
But I prayed concerning you, that your trust may not fail; and you, when you have turned back, strengthen your siblings.
He said to him, Lord, with you I am ready to go both to prison and to death.
He said, I tell you, Peter, a rooster will not sound today until three times you deny knowing me.
He said to them, When I sent you without purse, bag, and sandals, did you lack anything? They said, Nothing.
He said to them, But now, the one having a purse, take it, likewise a bag; and the one not having, sell one's cloak and buy a sword.
For I say to you that this written thing must be completed in me: And he was counted with lawless ones. For indeed what concerns me has its completion.
They said, Lord, look, here are two swords. He said to them, It is enough.
Going out, he went according to custom to the Mount of Olives, and the disciples followed him.
Arriving at the place, he said to them, Pray not to enter testing.
He withdrew from them about a stone's throw, knelt down, and prayed,
Father, if you are willing, take this cup away from me; yet not my will, but yours happen.
[A messenger from heaven appeared to him, strengthening him.]
[Being in agony, he prayed more earnestly, and his sweat became like drops of blood falling to the ground.]
Rising from prayer, coming to the disciples, he found them sleeping from grief.
He said to them, Why are you sleeping? Rise and pray, so that you may not enter testing.
While he was still speaking, look, a crowd, and the one called Judas, one of the twelve, was going before them. He drew near to Jesus to kiss him.
Jesus said to him, Judas, do you hand over the Son of Humanity with a kiss?
Those around him, seeing what would be, said, Lord, shall we strike with a sword?
A certain one of them struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his right ear.
Jesus answered, Permit even this. Touching the ear, he healed him.
Jesus said to the chief priests, officers of the temple, and elders who had come against him, Have you come out as against a robber, with swords and clubs?
Day by day, while I was with you in the temple, you did not stretch out hands against me; but this is your hour and the authority of darkness.
Seizing him, they led him away and brought him into the high priest's house. Peter was following from far away.
They kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat together; Peter sat among them.
A certain servant girl, seeing him sitting by the light and looking closely at him, said, This one also was with him.
But he denied it, saying, Woman, I do not know him.
After a little while, another seeing him said, You also are one of them. But Peter said, Human one, I am not.
After about one hour had passed, another insisted, saying, In truth this one also was with him, for he is a Galilean.
Peter said, Human one, I do not know what you say. Immediately, while he was still speaking, a rooster sounded.
The Lord turned and looked at Peter. Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said to him, Before a rooster sounds today, you will deny me three times.
Going out, he wept bitterly.
The men holding him were mocking and beating him.
Covering him, they were questioning him, saying, Prophesy: who is the one who struck you?
They spoke many other blasphemous things against him.
When day came, the council of elders of the people gathered, both chief priests and scribes, and they led him into their council.
They said, If you are the Messiah, tell us. He said to them, If I tell you, you will certainly not trust.
And if I question, you will certainly not answer.
But from now on the Son of Humanity will be seated at the right hand of the power of the Divine.
They all said, Are you then the Son of the Divine? He said to them, You say that I am.
They said, What further need do we have of testimony? For we ourselves heard from his mouth.
